problem_statement: Many individuals struggle with effectively managing their personal finances, understanding their spending habits, and adhering to set budgets. This often leads to overspending, lack of financial awareness, and difficulty in achieving financial goals.
solution_overview: Develop a user-friendly mobile application that allows individuals to track their personal expenses, categorize spending, visualize financial data through monthly graphs, set spending budgets, and receive automated notifications when budgets are exceeded.
functional_requirements: Users must be able to log individual expenses with details such as amount, date, and description.
The application must allow users to assign expenses to predefined or custom categories (e.g., Food, Transport, Entertainment).
The application must display a monthly summary of expenses, presented visually using graphs (e.g., pie charts, bar graphs).
Users must be able to set a budget for overall spending or for specific categories over a defined period (e.g., monthly).
The system must send notifications to the user when their spending approaches or exceeds a set budget.
The application's user interface and content must be presented in English.
non_functional_requirements: The application must have an intuitive and user-friendly interface for easy expense logging and data visualization.
The application must be performant, ensuring quick data entry and responsive loading of charts and summaries.
User financial data must be securely stored and protected with appropriate privacy measures.
The application should be reliable, ensuring data integrity and availability.
The application should be cross-platform (iOS and Android) for broader accessibility (inferred for 'app').
The application should be maintainable and scalable for future enhancements (e.g., cloud sync, advanced reporting).
tech_stack_recommendation: Mobile Development Framework: Flutter or React Native (for cross-platform compatibility)
Local Database: SQLite (for on-device data storage)
Backend (Optional, for cloud sync/backup): Node.js with Express.js or Python with Django/Flask
Cloud Database (Optional, for backend): PostgreSQL or MongoDB
UI/UX Design Libraries: Material Design or Cupertino (depending on chosen framework)
